name: kitdatepicker
description: Project containing your PowerApps Component Framework (PCF) control.

comments.virtual: This is a virtual control: the platform hands it the host's own React and Fluent at runtime (the manifest's platform-library lines), so neither is bundled. React and Fluent live in devDependencies purely for compile and local harness use, React at the platform version, Fluent at the kit's API floor (see pcfs/platform-floor.json), which is higher than the version the manifest declares: the manifest number is only what the org must ACCEPT at import (the runtime serves its current copy), while the devDependency records the oldest delivery the kit's code actually works against. Compiling against it keeps the control off APIs an older target org might not serve.
comments.compat: The date and time picker compat packages are the one exception: the platform Fluent library does not carry them (checked against the live org's Fluent v9 platform module, which has no DatePicker or TimePicker), so they stay real dependencies and ride in the bundle. Their internal focus-management chain still has to match the host's shared tabster instance on window, so the overrides keep pinning it; read the host's live version (window.__tabsterInstance._version) before re-pinning after a platform bump.
